programme.title: Lucha interna: Parte II
programme.desc: Le dan trato preferencial a Lucha y hasta un famoso narcotraficante se pone a sus órdenes porque cree que mató a un delincuente. Albertano se corta un mechón por el cual le dan mucho dinero. Lucha se enoja muchísimo al enterarse.

programme.title: Una patita de conejo fresca
programme.desc: Albertano le manda a María la llave de su cuarto para que saque sus cosas, pero ella entiende le pide que se mude con él, con su llave tiene una pata de conejo de la buena suerte pero al ensuciarse, María la tira y le compra una pata nueva.

programme.title: Sed de agua
programme.desc: Doña Lucha aprovecha el corte de agua en la colonia para vender unas cubetas que tenía apartadas. María se pone celosa de la nueva ayudante del equipo de futbol, Citlali, mientras que Albertano se la pasa en la banca por ser mal jugador.

programme.title: Patadas de ahogada
programme.desc: Es el cumpleaños de Albertano y doña Lucha le organiza una fiesta, María se pinta el cabello y se pone su más lindo vestido para recuperar a Albertano, le dedica canciones compuestas por ella y le recita un poema. Doña Lucha se siente mal.

programme.title: Angeloterapeuta
programme.desc: Albertano le pide tiempo a María para pensar en el futuro de su relación y ella va con un brujo para pedirle ayuda. Doña Lucha intenta hablar con el chino sobre sexualidad, por lo que van a la fonda de Don Carmelo, donde ella termina ebria.

programme.title: Sexy calendario
programme.desc: A Doña Lucha se le ocurre hacer un calendario sexy con todas sus vecinas, pero no se vende como ella esperaba. María se pone colágeno en los labios para conquistar a Albertano.

programme.title: Arturo sastre
programme.desc: Arturo takes out his old sewing machine to dedicate himself to sewing. Vanesa brings a rooster with Alzheimer's into the building that crows all day.

programme.title: El trabajo de Alejandra
programme.desc: The López Pérez need an extra income of money and Alejandra goes out to look for a job. Benito meets a friend from the castings and invites him to the apartment.

programme.title: La gotera
programme.desc: A leak in the López Pérez bathroom causes leaks in Pedro's apartment. Benito and Liz go to a casting for the musical Grease.

programme.title: Mi amiga Virginia
programme.desc: Virginia is left homeless and jobless. Germán decides to give her asylum and she becomes everyone's best friend. Luis signs up for Zumba classes online.

programme.title: Fiesta de casados
programme.desc: Luis feels lonely and Silvia encourages him to organize a meeting in the apartment. Luis invites the married men in the building. Vanesa entrusts Germán with a little bird with behavior problems.

programme.title: San Antonio
programme.desc: Germán and Jorjáis accidentally break Silvia's San Antonio statue. The López Pérez family complain about the high water consumption in the building.

programme.title: No se peleen
programme.desc: Germán and Jorjáis fight over Borre's last taco and they stop talking. Vanesa leaves Benito in charge of a cat.

programme.title: El álbum
programme.desc: Germán and Jorjáis find a photo album in the garbage and through it they remember the best of the worst of the neighbors.

programme.title: Germán por un día
programme.desc: Germán gets sick and the neighbors decide to help him with the building work. Magdalena invents a trip to Paris to show off to her friend.

programme.title: Embarazo
programme.desc: Germán has the task of planting small trees that the mayor's office has given them. Someone in the building is pregnant.

programme.title: ¡Voy a ser papá!
programme.desc: Silvia le confiesa a Luis que está embarazada y él comienza a comprar muchas cosas para el bebé. Magdalena y su familia se vuelven ricos y comienzan a derrochar su dinero.

programme.title: La casa de las Lomas
programme.desc: Magdalena les dice a los vecinos que ella y su familia se mudarán a Las Lomas y le pide a Luis comprarle una mansión.

programme.title: Perdimos todo
programme.desc: Luis comienza a molestar a Silvia todo el tiempo queriendo controlar lo que hace para cuidar del bebé. Magdalena invierte todo su dinero en una empresa fantasma y pierde todo.

programme.title: Luisito, corazón de plomo
programme.desc: Magda le dice a Luis que no pueden mudarse a su mansión y le pide de vuelta las llaves del departamento, pero él se niega a dejarlos vivir en en edificio otra vez y todos los vecinos tratan de convencerlo de darles otra oportunidad.

programme.title: El muñeco vudú
programme.desc: Germán y Jorjáis encuentran un muñeco vudú, asumen que alguien está haciendo brujería en el edificio. Pedro, Cris y Vanesa tienen problemas con el WiFi de su departamento.

programme.title: Síntomas empáticos
programme.desc: Luis descubre que es él (y no Silvia), quien tiene los síntomas del embarazo. Los vecinos encuentran en la calle carteles pegados de: "Se busca a Jorjáis".

programme.title: La hija de Jorjais
programme.desc: Una mujer le pregunta a Germán sobre el paradero de Jorjáis, y él se sorprende al descubrir que ella es su hija. Vanessa cree que tiene la maldición de la soltería.

programme.title: Niño o niña
programme.desc: Luis ya no sabe que hacer con Silvia porque tiene muchos cambios de humor por el embarazo, pero sus vecinos lo convencen de darle una sorpresa que jamás olvidará.

programme.title: Sortilegio
programme.desc: Alejandro returns home after a terrible accident to discover that he is married to Maria Jose, a poor, innocent girl who was deceived by Alejandro's brother Bruno. Bruno used Alejandro's name to marry Maria Jose, soon after he sent to kill his brother in hopes that Maria Jose would inherit Alejandro's fortune. But when Maria Jose arrives at the mansion she realizes that Bruno lied to her and used her. She is also scared when Alejandro is found alive and returns home. Doctors tell Alejandro that he suffers from memory loss but he is convinced he never met Maria Jose before and goes in search of the truth, but during this time and Maria Jose actually fall in love, while Bruno and Maura (Alejandro's ex-girlfriend) plot to break them up. Meanwhile, the brothers' mother Victoria has fallen in love with Fernando--Alejandro's best friend, so closer to his age than hers.

programme.title: Forces of the Wild I: In the Beginning
programme.desc: In the Beginning is the story of the origin of our solar system and the formation of our planet. Gorgeous visual images clearly illustrate the long geological history of ice and fire which formed the Earth’s crust, spawned volcanoes, created our atmosphere, turned our sky blue, and brought forth such wonders as the Earth’s magnetic field and the aurora borealis.

programme.title: Forces of the Wild II: Perpetual Motion
programme.desc: Perpetual Motion explores two great agents of creation and destruction – the atmosphere and the oceans. Together they create the patterns of life from rainforest to desert – and also produce the furies of hurricanes and tornadoes.

programme.title: Forces of the Wild III: Heavenly Partners
programme.desc: Heavenly Partners investigates the Earth’s most important neighbors the sun and the moon. These control the seasons and the tides, the very rhythms of life from the high reaches of the Arctic to the super-heated tropics.

programme.title: Forces of the Wild V: Playing with Fire
programme.desc: Playing with Fire looks at the techniques that have been developed to probe our planet, and how such techniques have both revolutionized our understanding of the world and allowed us to bring the dramatic images of Forces of the Wild to the TV screen. Throughout the film, ‘behind the scenes’ footage is combined with comment from key scientists in each field.

During the annual pig roundup, Steve learns about the ecological impact and challenges of controlling the hog population, providing a deeper understanding of the complexities of hunting and wildlife management in the Lone Star State.

programme.desc: Steven Rinella heads deep into Montana's rugged mountains to hunt black bears, reflecting on two decades of memories in this harsh yet captivating landscape. Joined by memories of his brother and their youthful adventures, Steve navigates grizzly country, contemplates the passage of time, and explores the evolving nature of hunting. Through encounters with both bears and the terrain, Steve seeks not just game but a connection to the past and the lessons it holds.

programme.title: Archie Goes Too Far
programme.desc: Archie is searching the house for a magazine to prove to Mike that he was right concerning a debate that they are having. However, Archie oversteps his boundaries and searches Mike's room where he discovers a poem that Mike once wrote. This leads to a huge argument concerning privacy and Mike storms out to Lionel's while Gloria and Edith storm out and have a slumber party at the apartment of one of Gloria's friends.

programme.title: Class Reunion
programme.desc: Edith gets an invitation to attend her 30th high school class reunion, but when Archie refuses to escort her, she decides not to go. When her Cousin Amelia shows up to question Edith about her decision, Edith learns that an old flame, Buck, will be attending the reunion. Suddenly, Archie decides that he would like to go. However, when Buck does finally show up, Archie is in for a surprise since the once good looking track star seems to have lost his good looks.

programme.title: The Threat
programme.desc: Archie gets word that an old Army buddy who once saved his life is coming to town with his wife, Bobbi Jo. Archie offers to have them stay at his place. When Bobbi Jo arrives early, Archie gets an eyeful of the voluptuous woman and his guilt over his lust begins to show through to Mike and Gloria. Edith overhears a conversation between Archie, Mike and Gloria and out of concern for her marriage, makes a pivotal decision.

programme.title: Lionel Steps Out
programme.desc: Archie's niece has been visiting the Bunkers and having a great time. She's even made friends with Lionel and loves going out on the town with him. However, a picture taken of the two of them becomes the thing that clues Archie in on their blossoming relationship and he hits the roof, citing that people should stick to their own race.

programme.title: The Bunkers And The Swingers
programme.desc: Edith finds a magazine on the subway and reads an ad in the "swap" section. Not realizing what is actually being asked for, Edith assumes the couple is looking for friendship. Mike and Gloria, in a rush, try to explain what they really want. However, it isn't until the couple is actually standing in the Bunker's living room that Edith realizes, with a little help from Louise, that they are "swingers" and they want to swap with Archie and Edith.

programme.title: Mike's Appendix
programme.desc: Both Archie and Gloria come home in bad moods after a hard day's work. This leads to yet another argument over women's liberation. Meanwhile, Mike has been having a pain in the side and the doctor tells him that he needs to have an appendectomy. This leads Archie and Gloria's argument to the proficiency of women doctors. Mike would rather have a more expensive male doctor rather than a cheaper woman doctor.

programme.title: Edith Flips Her Wig
programme.desc: Edith comes home from shopping in a terrible state; it seems she had been arrested for shoplifting. Apparently, she had walked out of a store, wearing a wig, while trying to give bus directions to a woman. Edith becomes convinced that she has become a kleptomaniac, after finding that she also "stole" a necklace. Edith begins to obsess and Mike suggests that she see a psychiatrist, however, Edith has something in mind herself; she visits a priest.

programme.title: The Locket
programme.desc: Edith's antique locket is missing and Archie sees it as a way to collect from the insurance company to buy a new television set. Confident of getting the insurance money, Archie orders a new color set, only to have an insurance adjuster arrive at the house to question the claim.

programme.title: Mike Comes Into Money
programme.desc: Mike comes into some money when his Uncle Alex dies and leaves him $275. Archie tries to persuade Mike into giving the money to him for room and board, but Mike would rather give it to George McGovern's presidential campaign. This sparks a big feud, leading to a dinner separation: Gloria and Mike buy and cook their own food while Edith and Archie do the same.

programme.title: Flashback: Mike And Gloria's Wedding
programme.desc: Edith is in a celebratory mood for Mike and Gloria's second wedding anniversary. While sitting around the dinner table enjoying a special cake, the family recalls Mike and Gloria's wedding. They also recall how Mike's Uncle Casimir met Archie and how they butted heads over who would perform the ceremony.

programme.title: Flashback: Mike And Gloria's Wedding - Pt. Ii
programme.desc: The family continues to reminisce on Mike and Gloria's second wedding anniversary and they recall the actual day Mike and Gloria got married. As Archie and Mike's Uncle Casimir continue to butt heads, Edith must gather up strength to tell Gloria about 'the birds and the bees'.

programme.title: Edith's Winning Ticket
programme.desc: When Edith finds some old lottery tickets that she has totally forgotten about, Archie hits the roof claiming she was wasting money. However, Archie's attitude quickly changes when the family discovers that one of the tickets is a winner, but there is a catch: Edith claims the tickets don't belong to her, but belong to George Jefferson.

programme.title: Archie Is Branded
programme.desc: One morning Archie gets up, goes to his front door to get the newspaper and is shocked to discover that someone has painted a swastika on his front door. Archie soon gets into a debate with Mike as to who could have painted it and why. As Archie is calling the police to report it, the Bunker home gets a visit from a man from a Hebrew vigilante group who wants to plan some retaliation.

programme.title: Archie And The Bowling Team
programme.desc: Archie comes home in a fantastic mood for a change and it's all because he has the chance to possibly join a prestigious bowling team, the Cannonballers. After waiting six years, it seems that there is finally a vacancy on the team and Archie is planning to try out, even though the team practices discrimination in admitting members.

programme.title: Archie In The Hospital
programme.desc: Archie has been suffering from a terrible pain in his back and has become a terrible pain to Edith, who has been waiting on him hand and foot. When the doctor takes a look at him, he orders Archie to be admitted to the hospital for tests. This is where Archie befriends the other patient in his room, but because of a partition, Archie doesn't realize the man is black.

programme.title: Oh Say Can You See

programme.title: Pod Save America (Trump Sabotages Trump Agenda)
programme.desc: Donald Trump cancels the signing of a landmark bipartisan housing bill at the last minute, saying he won't approve it until Congress passes the SAVE America Act, which would require proof of citizenship to register to vote and photo ID to cast a ballot. Jon and Dan react to the President's decision and the Republican caucus's angry reaction to it, then turn to Tuesday's primary elections, where three DSA-aligned candidates won in New York City, and discuss JD Vance's attempts to charm Rupert Murdoch … and his own wife. Then, Gina Hinojosa, the Democratic gubernatorial nominee in Texas, stops by the studio to talk to Jon about what it'll take to unseat Governor Greg Abbott.

programme.title: What a Day (JD Vance Takes On Iran Negotiations)
programme.desc:
Vice President JD Vance has been given a job no one really wants: ending the incredibly unpopular Iran war. He’s been tasked with leading negotiations with Iranian counterparts to find a lasting solution that he thinks could QUOTE “transform the Middle East.” But there are many, many obstacles in his way. And as the US and Iran attempt to hammer out not just an end to the war, but a new nuclear deal, President Trump seems markedly uninterested in the whole thing. So for more on the mess that is trying to end the Iran war, we spoke with Tommy Vietor. He’s the co-host of Crooked Media’s Pod Save the World.
And in headlines, Moscow’s Defense Ministry says Ukraine launched a major Thursday night attack, ex-national security adviser John Bolton pleads guilty to a single count of illegally retaining national defense information, and New York City approves a rent freeze on one- and two-year leases for people living in about 1 million rent-stabilized apartments.

programme.title: What a Day (SCOTUS: Don't Give US Your Huddled Masses)
programme.desc:
As so many World Cup fans are discovering ranch dressing and enjoying their visits to the U.S., the Supreme Court left a very different message for people who come to the U.S. for safety and protection: leave. In two 6-3 decisions today, the Supreme Court ruled that the Trump Administration can block asylum seekers at the border and withdraw deportation protections from immigrants fleeing violence and war in their home countries. For more on how the Court’s decisions will shape future U.S. immigration policy, we spoke to Andrea Flores. She’s a former Homeland Security official under Presidents Barack Obama and Joe Biden, and the founder of Securing America’s Promise, a pro-immigration newsletter.
And in headlines, the White House formally requests $87.6 billion mostly to replenish the Pentagon , what the U.S. is doing to help after deadly earthquakes in Venezuela, and a new AP-NORC survey says most Americans are inundated with scam attempts on a daily basis. But you probably already knew that — just check your texts.

programme.title: What a Day (Trump, GOP Shouting Match)
programme.desc: President Trump has one clear talent: pissing off his purported allies, like when he was supposed to sign a big bipartisan housing bill. Republicans were hoping to campaign on the legislation and show that they care about the number one concern of American voters: the high cost of living. But Donald Trump doesn't care. Wednesday morning, he abruptly canceled the signing ceremony without telling Republican members of Congress, declaring he won't sign the bill until Congress passes the SAVE America Act. This massive federal elections bill would require Americans to show their passport or birth certificate to vote. To talk more about how Trump seems determined to derail Congress, even when it hurts his own party, we spoke with Maryland Democratic Senator Chris Van Hollen. And in headlines, the U.S. military reinstates flu shot requirements for recruits after an outbreak at a Texas Air Force base, New York City Mayor Zohran Mamdani won big in Tuesday’s primary, and a federal judge permanently bars the Trump administration from implementing most of Trump’s first executive order on elections.

programme.title: Pod Save America (Trump Sabotages Trump Agenda)
programme.desc: Donald Trump cancels the signing of a landmark bipartisan housing bill at the last minute, saying he won't approve it until Congress passes the SAVE America Act, which would require proof of citizenship to register to vote and photo ID to cast a ballot. Jon and Dan react to the President's decision and the Republican caucus's angry reaction to it, then turn to Tuesday's primary elections, where three DSA-aligned candidates won in New York City, and discuss JD Vance's attempts to charm Rupert Murdoch … and his own wife. Then, Gina Hinojosa, the Democratic gubernatorial nominee in Texas, stops by the studio to talk to Jon about what it'll take to unseat Governor Greg Abbott.

programme.title: Lovett or Leave It (New York Sees Red)
programme.desc: Mamdani's candidates win big in New York. Pesticides win big at the Supreme Court. And we all win when John Stamos stops by to talk Beach Boys, Full House, and why his dad finally let him quit flipping burgers. Then Jodie-Turner Smith waxes philosophical about aliens, accents, Michael Fassbender, and a gripping defense of toe shoes, courtesy of the Egg of Truth. And don’t worry, baby: we have plenty of Second Thoughts to go around.

programme.title: This F*cking Guy (Bari Weiss: Everything You Didn't Know About Her Sh*tty Past)
programme.desc: In our 40th episode of This F*cking Guy, Erin dives deep into the origins of anti-woke crusader turned CBS News Editor-in-Chief Bari Weiss. From her origins as an antagonizing student at Columbia University to a career of ill researched op-eds and launching her own media company, to running CBS News and her relentless campaign to promote Zionism, this may be our most manipulative guy yet!

programme.title: Offline with Jon Favreau (What Is A Human For?)
programme.desc: AI is creating a crisis of agency where people are becoming paranoid that they’re being manipulated, suckered, and ultimately replaced. Charlie Warzel joins Offline to elaborate on his Atlantic essay, “The Feeling of Control Slipping Away,” which illustrates the myriad ways AI is driving people insane. He and Jon talk about whether human creativity is endangered, if AI is anything more than a corporate black-box, and what it means to be human when a robot can do everything better than you. They also discuss Elon Musk's SpaceX IPO, what its valuation means and Musk's efforts to rewrite DOGE's devastating legacy, as well as his own.

programme.title: This F*cking Guy (Laura Loomer: Everything You Didn't Know About Her Sh*tty Past)
programme.desc: In our 34th episode of This F*cking Guy, Erin and Alyssa dive deep into the past of the unhinged MAGA clown, Laura Loomer. From filming undercover for Project Veritas, to cozying up to eco-fascists, to melting down over her Twitter ban, to being Trump’s number 1 fan girl, this crazy b*tch may be our nuttiest guy yet!

programme.title: A Square Is Not Round
programme.desc: Everything is topsy-turvy and henhouse hijinks ensue when one of the Douglas' hens is found to be laying square eggs! News of this kind gets around quickly, and the townsfolk all get in on the race to capitalize on the impressive novelty.

programme.title: An Old Fashioned Christmas
programme.desc: Oliver is absolutely determined to have a truly old-fashioned Christmas, including decorating his own farm-grown tree. But warm weather stands in the way and, he discovers, the residents of Hooterville aren't exactly as traditional as he thought.

programme.title: Never Trust A Little Old Lady
programme.desc: In true Hooterville fashion, Oliver (Eddie Albert) discovers that the town is vehemently split as to which weather reporter they trust. As debates rage over which forecast is correct, Oliver plants his tomato crop, hoping for the best.

programme.title: School Days
programme.desc: After another disastrous effort from Lisa in the kitchen, Oliver implores her to take a home economics class at the local high school. Before she knows it, Lisa is a fully enrolled student...the likes of which Hooterville High has never quite seen.

programme.title: His Honor
programme.desc: When the Hooterville City Council appoints Oliver (Eddie Albert) to judge the apple competition at the annual fair, he mistakes the honor for a real judgeship and prepares for his time on the bench! But a run-in with the real law clarifies matters.

programme.title: It's So Peaceful In The Country
programme.desc: Lisa visits Oliver's mother who is recovering from an illness. The doctor advises that she rest in a peaceful environment...and thinks that Hooterville sounds absolutely ideal! Of course, he doesn't know Hooterville, where peace is never�very close.

programme.title: Exodus To Bleedswell
programme.desc: When Oliver (Eddie Albert) discovers that Hooterville has a still-valid contract to build war planes, he spearheads the opening of new government defense plant. But as chaos ensues, Oliver soon wonders if the move was a big mistake after all.

programme.title: It's Human To Be Humane
programme.desc: Lisa is appointed as head of Hooterville's new "Human Humane" committee. A perfect fit for animal-lover Lisa, and a relief from her boredom in the countryside, she soon has a menagerie of both farm and exotic animals living at Green Acres.

programme.title: Never Take Your Wife To A Convention
programme.desc: Oliver takes Lisa along to an agricultural convention. There, Lisa bonds with an ex-gangster couple, who insist upon teaching the Douglas's some of their shortcuts to successful farming. It turns out to be an offer that Oliver can not refuse.

programme.title: The Computer Age
programme.desc: Oliver champions agri-tech as way to increase productivity on the farm, but when a computer gives its assessment of the Douglas' marriage, Lisa (Eva Gabor) begins to question her union with Oliver. It's bits versus bytes in Green Acres!

programme.title: Lisa's Vegetable Garden
programme.desc: Determined to teach his spendthrift wife Lisa how to save money, Oliver insists that she learn about farming...by growing her own vegetables! In her own inimitable fashion, armed with useless pamphlets from Mr. Kimball, Lisa sets to tilling!

programme.title: The Saucer Season
programme.desc: When handyman Eb (Tom Lester) reports he has seen little green men piloting flying saucers, everyone in Hooterville takes him at his word�even the Air Force�and soon the Douglas farm becomes the Roswell of the greater Pixley area!

programme.title: Kimball Gets Fired
programme.desc: Ralph (Mary Grace Canfield) is distraught when her beloved Mr. Kimball, is fired as the county agricultural agent. Out of a job, Kimball (Alvy Moore) no longer has money enough to marry the lady-carpenter-in-waiting! But wait�did he even want to?

programme.title: The Vulgar Ring Story
programme.desc: Lisa weaves the tale of her great, great grandmother, the Queen of the Gypsies, to explain a bizarre family tradition: a woman in her family must marry an American -- but only every fourth generation -- in order to gain a special inheritance.

programme.title: Who's Lisa
programme.desc: After Oliver (Eddie Albert) accidentally drops a hammer on her head, Lisa (Eva Gabor) develops a case amnesia, with no idea who she is or why she is on a farm in Hooterville�and she adamantly refuses to believe that Oliver is her husband!

programme.title: Music To Milk By
programme.desc: Nothing gets done on the Douglas farm when Oliver (Eddie Albert) gifts Eb (Tom Lester) a top-of-the-line radio for his birthday. Letting nothing stop him, Eb (Tom Lester) enters a radio contest...even when Eleanor the cow swallows the radio whole.

programme.title: The Man For The Job
programme.desc: It's a very official third season premiere for Green Acres when the retirement of a state senator leaves Hooterville considering its best candidate for the job. Of course, they turn to their beloved lawyer-come-farmer Oliver Douglas (Eddie Albert).

programme.title: Ophelia Finds Romance
programme.desc: Ophelia Frump is madly in love with Horatio Bartholomew, the man who has everything, except the truth about her relatives. Morticia, not convinced by Bartholomew, persuades playboy Cousin Itt to stir the embers of his past romance with Ophelia.

programme.title: Pugsley's Allowance
programme.desc: Pugsley shocks his parents when he gives up his new atomic reactor as kid stuff and announces that he wants to find his own job. His first try is as a medical assistant to Dr. Bird. He turns up, scalpel in hand, ready to help during an operation.

programme.title: Happy Birthday, Grandma Frump
programme.desc: Grandma Frump (guest star Margaret Hamilton) comes to the mistaken conclusion that her daughter, Morticia (Carolyn Jones), and Gomez (John Astin) plan to pack her off to an old folks home. Actually, they plan to send her to a beauty farm.

programme.title: Morticia, The Decorator
programme.desc: Morticia, in order to be part of the National Beautification Program, decides to become a home decorator and offers her services to the neighbors. Next door neighbor, and insurance agent, Joe Digby hires Morticia in hopes of selling them insurance.

programme.title: Ophelia Visits Morticia
programme.desc: Ophelia taunts Uncle Fester into announcing that he is going to join the Peace Corps. Fester feels he's a cinch to be accepted because Gomez plans to pull strings in Washington on his behalf. The entire family agrees to help him study for the exam.

programme.title: Addams Cum Laude
programme.desc: Allyn Joslyn guest-stars as Sam Hilliard, a former truant officer who now heads a private school where Wednesday and Pugsley are enrolled. When Hilliard breaks under the strain of dealing with the Addams children, Gomez buys the school.

programme.title: Cat Addams
programme.desc: Dr. Marvin P. Gunderson is summoned to examine Kitty Kat, the ailing lion pet of the Addams Family. Morticia decides that it is the doctor who needs help. To build his confidence, human members of the family pretend to be ill so he can affect cures.

programme.title: Lurch's Little Helper
programme.desc: Morticia's concern that Lurch is overworked leads Gomez to the operating table to assemble a custom-built secondary butler. Lurch lets the robot do all the work and then begins to feel that he isn't needed anymore.

programme.title: The Addams Policy
programme.desc: When Uncle Fester incinerates the family's stuffed bear with his flamethrower, Morticia reports the accident to the insurance company. Mr. Henson, the head of the company, sends his assistant, Mr. Digby, to handle the case.

programme.title: Lurch's Grand Romance
programme.desc: Lurch suffers from unrequited love over Tiny Trivia (guest star Diane Jergens), Morticia's visiting school chum who only has eyes for show business. The entire family decides to try to turn their stolid butler in to a new personality.

programme.title: Ophelia's Career
programme.desc: When Ophelia laments that she has been jilted, Morticia suggests a career as an alternative to marriage. She tries chemistry first (with explosive results) then turns to operatic singing. Cousin Itt is summoned to coach her to stardom.

programme.title: The Addams Family Goes To School
programme.desc: Morticia and Gomez decide to keep Wednesday and Pugsley home from school after Morticia sees the fairy tales they have to read. When the truant officer comes to investigate, he is shocked at what he finds.

programme.title: Morticia And The Psychiatrist
programme.desc: Morticia calls a psychiatrist after she fears Pugsley is becoming normal after he abandons his pet octopus for a puppy and is seen wearing a Boy Scout uniform, playing with a baseball bat, and helping an old lady cross the street.

programme.title: Fester's Punctured Romance
programme.desc: Uncle Fester, who has become lonely, convinces Gomez and Morticia that it is time for him to find a bride. He decides to put an ad in the newspaper, looking for love. But things go awry when a door-to-door saleswoman comes by.

programme.title: Gomez, The Politician
programme.desc: Election fever grips the Addams household, as Gomez (John Astin) supports the council candidate whose campaign promise is to drain the town's disgusting bogs. Morticia (Carolyn Jones), who likes "the lovely bogs," labels the candidate an extremist.

programme.title: The Addams Family Tree
programme.desc: Pugsley and Wednesday start a row at a party when their gift of a tarantula upsets Harold, the birthday boy. Harold's parents insist that the Addams children lack breeding and, therefore, are no longer allowed to play with their son.

programme.title: Morticia Joins The Ladies League
programme.desc: After Pugsley befriends Gorgo, a circus gorilla, Gorgo decides to follow Pugsley and Gomez home, where he is accepted as one of the family. But Gorgo gives Morticia's tea guests, the Ladies League members, quite a shock.

programme.title: Julia Stuntz

programme.title: More Friends Speak Out
programme.desc: Another close friend of Ana Abulaban took the stand. Prosecutors wanted to portray the defendant, Ali Abulaban, as an abusive, jealous, out-of-control husband whose behavior led him to plot and kill his wife, Ana, and her friend, Rayburn Barron.

programme.title: Ongoing Domestic Abuse

programme.title: Staying In The Abusive Relationship
programme.desc: Friends of Ana Abulaban told jurors in her husband's murder trial that she kept giving Ali second chances. This was after they said she had bruises and faced psychological torment.

programme.title: The Extramarital Affair
programme.desc: The defendant, Ali Abulaban, met another woman in the elevator of his apartment building. Ashley Sanchez testified that she had sex with him just weeks before the fatal shootings of his wife Ana and her friend, Rayburn Barron.

programme.title: How To Find Direction When Nothing Feels Right
programme.desc: From 6× Mr. Olympia to full-time business owner and stay-at-home dad, it’s safe to say Chris Bumstead’s been enjoying retired life. So what’s he been up to this past year, what’s changed most for him, and what’s he looking forward to next? Expect to learn how retirement is going for Cbum, if Cbum would have retired last year even if he didn’t win, how Chris learned to sit and process his emotions, if Cbum regrets retirement and if he misses the competing yet, how Chris plans on leveling up for baby Bumstead number two, he hardest part of losing that “Mr. Olympia structure” in daily life, if Chris ever plans on returning the stage again, and much more…

programme.title: Comforting Truths About Human Nature
programme.desc: How can we truly understand ourselves? Most of us either ignore our emotions or overthink them, turning simple feelings into complex puzzles. So how do we navigate the minefield of emotion to grow and become better? Expect to learn where self-esteem really comes from, why criticism hurts so much, where imposter syndrome comes from and how to overcome it, if hustle culture is a modern phenomenon or is this something new, why we tend to intellectualise our emotions, why we have existential crisis’ and how to navigate them, why online dating is so miserable for so many people and much more…

programme.title: How to Treat Men Better
programme.desc: Are modern gender dynamics leaving men feeling ‘castrated’? While men are often blamed for today’s social problems, far less attention is given to how shifting expectations of women and relationships may also shape male identity. What role do women play in this dynamic—and how can it be improved? Expect to learn how important it is for women to seem impressed or giddy or appreciative of their partner, how women can appear more receptive or appreciative, why men are being emasculated by women, how women can stop castrating men and much more…

programme.title: How to Defeat Your Stress, Anxiety & Inaction
programme.desc: If motivation can’t be trusted, what should you rely on instead? Bestselling author and top podcaster Mel Robbins explains why discipline and emotional control are the real keys to success, and how to build the mindset needed to follow through when it counts. Expect to learn why we cling to control even when we know it makes us miserable and why we focus on things we can't control, how to better deal with the judgment of others, where self-compassion comes from, how to get a grip on your anxiety and ADHD, what ""Let Them"" theory means and how to employ it, where self-compassion actually comes from and much more...
